description: Every concrete claim (obligations, deadlines, priorities, sources) traces to approved repo-local data — L2_facts/obligations.md, L2_facts/user-operating-profile.md, L2_facts/approved-information-sources.md, or notes the user supplied this turn. No fact appears that isn't backed by one of those. Pass only if the Situation and Next actions rest on real grounded items (e.g. the order-backlog / email-backlog obligations, the ranked Wanderland/Animayte pursuits), not plausible-sounding filler.

Where a fact is missing from the approved sources, the briefing says 'unknown' (or asks) rather than fabricating a name, date, number, status, or source. No invented meetings, contacts, dollar figures, or deadlines. Confidence is flagged when judgment is involved. Fabricating to fill a section is an automatic fail.

The 'Decision needed' section names a specific, answerable decision the user must own — a concrete fork with options or a clear yes/no — not a vague status line or a restated priority. If genuinely no decision is pending, it explicitly says so ('No decision needed this period') rather than leaving the section empty or padded.

Recommendation and Next actions are specific and executable: concrete verbs, named targets, and reversibility/undo noted where an action mutates state. A reader can act without re-deriving what to do. Reject generic advice ('keep making progress', 'stay on top of email').

The Risks section names real, briefing-specific risks or blockers tied to the surfaced items (e.g. backlog slipping, an irreversible/externally-visible step, a missing approval) — not boilerplate. Empty or 'no risks' is acceptable only when the items genuinely carry none.

High-signal and scannable: follows the Situation / Decision needed / Recommendation / Next actions / Risks / Follow-ups structure with no filler, no restated prompt, no generic moralizing. A reader can act without skimming past padding.
